items.title: 'I'm back' - record-breaking Ronaldo answers critics
items.summary: Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo provides the perfect response to those that doubted him as he becomes the first player to score at six World Cups.

items.title: Wayne Rooney claims Thomas Tuchel made public example of England star to send message to World Cup squad
items.summary: THOMAS TUCHEL deliberately made an example of Djed Spence during a recent training session, claims former England captain Wayne Rooney. items.title: 'Anxious moment for England' - should this have been Ghana penalty?
items.summary: England's Ezri Konsa makes a last-ditch attempt to stop Ghana's Prince Adu during their World Cup Group L match in Boston.

items.title: Rice remains positive after England draw with Ghana
items.summary: England midfielder Declan Rice says he remains positive despite being held to a 0-0 draw against Ghana in their World Cup Group L match in Boston.

items.title: England given reality check by stubborn Ghana to leave group in the balance

items.title: David Beckham is every England fan as he goes from blowing kiss to having head in hands in woeful World Cup Ghana draw
items.summary: DAVID BECKHAM summed up every England fan's emotions during the goalless draw with Ghana. The former England captain joined thousands of Three Lions supporters at the Boston Stadium on Tuesday. items.title: Fans claim Ghana should have been awarded penalty as Ezri Konsa avoids further disaster in England’s dire World Cup draw
items.summary: WORLD CUP fans reckon England defender Ezri Konsa was lucky not to concede a late penalty against Ghana. items.title: England player ratings: Anthony Gordon struggles will have Barcelona sweating but Elliot Anderson remains solid presence
items.summary: ENGLAND were brought back down to earth as they laboured to a 0-0 draw with Ghana. The Three Lions had gotten their World Cup campaign off to a perfect start last week by beating Croatia 4-2 in Texas. items.summary: AFTER Dallas delight, it was the Boston manglers. Lifeless and listless, limited and lame, the sort of England tournament performance we’ve witnessed too many times before. items.title: Jude Bellingham risks red card with gesture due to controversial rule in England’s World Cup clash vs Ghana
items.summary: JUDE BELLINGHAM risked a red card under a controversial new rule at the World Cup. The England star was left frustrated by Ghana in the Three Lions' second group game, which ended in a poor 0-0 draw. items.title: Biggest cheer of England vs Ghana first-half comes as Three Lions legend shown on big screen at World Cup clash
items.summary: SIR DAVID BECKHAM got the biggest cheer - but the England legend cut a frustrated figure as the Three Lions served up a drab draw in their World Cup showdown with Ghana. items.title: Jude Bellingham breaks Wayne Rooney’s 17-year-old England record just seconds into World Cup clash vs Ghana
items.summary: JUDE BELLINGHAM toppled Wayne Rooney and pinched an England record by lining up in the 0-0 draw with Ghana on Tuesday. items.title: Spence appears to snub Partey pre-match handshake
items.summary: Djed Spence appeared to not shake hands with Thomas Partey before England's match against Ghana.

items.title: Spence appears not to shake hands with Partey
items.summary: Footage circulating on social media appears to show England's Djed Spence not shaking hands with Ghana's Thomas Partey before their World Cup group match in Boston.

items.title: England held to goalless draw by Ghana
items.summary: England fail to repeat their performance that overpowered Croatia in their first World Cup game as they are held to a draw by a resolute Ghana at Boston Stadium.
